questions.text: Third equation of motion is independent of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Time does not appear in the third equation of motion, which involves final velocity, initial velocity, acceleration, and displacement. Therefore, the equation is independent of time.
Displacement is a crucial variable in the third equation of motion. It is necessary to determine the relationship between the velocities and acceleration, so it cannot be independent of the equation.
Velocity, including both initial and final velocities, is integral to the third equation of motion. It directly affects the calculation, making it dependent on the equation.
Acceleration is a fundamental part of the third equation of motion, impacting the change in velocity over a given displacement. Thus, it is not independent of the equation.

questions.explanation: The third equation of motion is expressed as vf2 - vi2 = 2as, where vf is the final velocity, vi is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and s is the displacement. Notably, this equation does not include the variable of time, which makes it independent of time. Other variables such as displacement, velocity, and acceleration are explicitly part of this equation, making them dependent on it.

questions.text: When we jump out a boat standing in water, it moves
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer because, according to Newton's third law of motion, the force exerted by the person jumping forward results in an equal and opposite reaction force on the boat, causing it to move backward.
This option is incorrect. The boat cannot move forward because the person jumping forward applies a backward force on the boat, resulting in it moving in the opposite direction.
This option is incorrect. Sideways movement would require a lateral force, which is not present in the action of jumping directly out of the boat.
This option is incorrect. The scenario clearly describes an action and reaction pair according to Newton's third law, which dictates that the boat will move backward.

questions.explanation: According to Newton's third law of motion, every action has an equal and opposite reaction. When a person jumps out of a boat in water, they exert a force on the boat in the opposite direction of their jump. This causes the boat to move backward. The backward movement is the reaction to the force exerted by the jumper. The other options are incorrect because they do not adhere to the principles of action and reaction forces as described by Newton's third law.

questions.text: Which of the following must always be true?I. If an object's acceleration is constant, then it must move in a straight line.II. If an object's acceleration is zero, then its speed must remain constant.III. If an object's speed remains constant, then its acceleration must be zero.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Statement I is false because constant acceleration does not imply linear motion; a projectile follows a curved path with constant acceleration. Statement II is true because zero acceleration implies no change in speed, in line with Newton's First Law.

Statement I is false as constant acceleration can occur in non-linear motion. Statement III is false because an object can maintain constant speed while changing direction, such as in circular motion, which involves acceleration.
Statement II is true; zero acceleration means the object's speed remains unchanged, consistent with Newton's First Law. This is the only statement that is universally true for all motions.
Statement III is false because an object moving at constant speed can still be accelerating if it changes direction, such as in circular motion where centripetal acceleration is present.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C: II only. Statement II correctly identifies that zero acceleration results in a constant speed, aligning with Newton's First Law that an object will maintain its state of motion unless acted upon by an external force. Statement I is incorrect because an object under constant acceleration can move along a curve, like in projectile motion. Statement III is incorrect because an object can have a constant speed but still accelerate if it's changing direction, as seen in circular motion where centripetal acceleration occurs.

questions.text: The quantitative measure of inertia of a body is its:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Mass is the quantitative measure of an object's inertia. It is a constant property that determines how much an object resists changes to its motion regardless of its environment.
Weight is the force exerted by gravity on an object. It varies with the gravitational field and is not a measure of inertia, as it depends on location.
Velocity is a vector quantity describing an object's speed and direction of motion. It does not relate to the object's resistance to changes in motion.
Momentum is the product of an object's mass and velocity. While mass is part of momentum, momentum itself is not a direct measure of inertia but rather a measure of motion.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Mass because it represents the quantitative measure of inertia, indicating an object's resistance to changes in its state of motion. Mass remains constant regardless of an object's location. Weight, in contrast, depends on gravitational pull and varies with location. Velocity describes how fast and in what direction an object moves, but it does not measure resistance to motion. Momentum combines mass and velocity to describe motion but is not solely a measure of inertia.

questions.text: A rocket works on basis of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Newton's first law addresses the concept of inertia, where an object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force. While important in understanding motion, it does not explain the propulsion mechanism of rockets, which involves interactions between forces.

Newton's second law establishes the relationship between force, mass, and acceleration, indicating how an object's motion changes under a net force. Although relevant to calculating the acceleration of a rocket, it does not directly describe the principle of propulsion through action and reaction.

Newton's third law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This principle is crucial for rocket propulsion: the expulsion of exhaust gases backward (action) results in a forward thrust (reaction), propelling the rocket.

Newton's gravitational law explains the attractive force between masses, such as the Earth and a rocket. While gravity is significant in determining a rocket's trajectory, it is not the mechanism by which a rocket propels itself forward.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Newton's third law of motion, which states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This principle is fundamental to rocket propulsion: the action of expelling exhaust gases downward produces an opposite reaction that propels the rocket upward.Newton's first law concerns inertia and does not involve the action-reaction pairs that drive rocket motion. Newton's second law describes how force acts on mass to produce acceleration, but it does not specifically explain the mechanism of action and reaction in propulsion. Newton's gravitational law explains how gravity affects the motion of objects, influencing trajectory but not providing the mechanism for rocket propulsion.

questions.text: In an inelastic collision, Momentum is _ and Kinetic Energy is _ respectively.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In an inelastic collision, momentum is conserved, meaning that the total momentum before and after the collision remains the same. However, kinetic energy is not conserved in an inelastic collision. Some of the kinetic energy is converted into other forms of energy, such as heat or deformation of the colliding objects. Therefore, the kinetic energy decreases in an inelastic collision.

questions.explanation: For elastic and inelastic collisions, momentum and total energy are always conserved provided no external force is applied; however, for inelastic collisions, kinetic energy is NOT conserved.

questions.text: Law of Conservation of Momentum states that total linear momentum of system remains constant if:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Balanced external forces are applied to a system.
options.explanation: This option is not correct. The conservation of momentum is not dependent on balanced external forces. Even if there are balanced forces, momentum can still be conserved as long as there are no net external forces acting on the system.

options.text: No external force is applied to a system.
options.explanation: This option is partially correct. The Law of Conservation of Momentum is most applicable when no external forces are applied to a closed system. In this case, the total linear momentum of the system remains constant.

options.text: Only linear force is applied in a system is zero.
options.explanation: This option is not correct. The presence or absence of linear forces alone does not determine the conservation of momentum. The key factor is the net external force acting on the system.

options.text: Sum of external forces are applied to a system is zero.
options.explanation: This option is correct. According to the Law of Conservation of Momentum, the total linear momentum of a closed system remains constant if the sum of external forces applied to the system is zero. This means that the net external force acting on the system is zero, and momentum is conserved.

questions.explanation: This is a statement of the conservation of linear momentum, thus, it is a fact. For the sake of explanation, an external force would disturb the equilibrium of the system and produce an acceleration in the objects being tested.

questions.text: A person is standing on a bus. When the bus starts moving forward suddenly:
questions.subject: Physics

questions.explanation: When the bus starts moving forward suddenly, the person inside the bus appears to move backward due to inertia. questions.explanation: The correct answer is C: Change in momentum. The other options are incorrect because: A) Angular momentum relates to rotational motion and does not stem from this equation; B) Force cannot equal the product of itself and time; D) Velocity is a measure of speed in a specific direction and does not directly relate to the force-time product.

questions.text: Decrease in velocity per unit time is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity per unit time. It can be positive (speeding up) or negative (slowing down). For example, when a car speeds up, its velocity increases and its acceleration is positive. When a car slows down, its velocity decreases and its acceleration is negative. Deceleration, on the other hand, is a type of acceleration that occurs when an object slows down. It is also known as negative acceleration. When an object decelerates, its velocity decreases and its acceleration is negative. So, a decrease in velocity per unit time is not acceleration because it is the opposite of acceleration. It is deceleration.
A decrease in velocity per unit time is not positive acceleration. It is the opposite of positive acceleration and is called deceleration.

Deceleration is the rate at which an object slows down. It is measured as the decrease in velocity per unit time, and is given by the formula: deceleration = (final velocity - initial velocity) / timewhere "final velocity" is the velocity of the object at the end of the time interval, "initial velocity" is the velocity of the object at the beginning of the time interval, and "time" is the duration of the interval.If the final velocity is less than the initial velocity, then the deceleration is negative, indicating that the object is slowing down. If the final velocity is greater than the initial velocity, then the deceleration is positive, indicating that the object is speeding up.

A decrease in velocity per unit time is not uniform acceleration. Uniform acceleration refers to a constant rate of change in velocity over time, either positive or negative. Deceleration, on the other hand, is not uniform acceleration because the rate at which the velocity decreases is not constant over time. The rate of deceleration may change depending on the object's initial velocity, its mass, and the forces acting on it.

questions.text: A paratrooper is falling down with uniform velocity and also rotating with a constant angular velocity of 0.2 rad/sec. The body satisfies _.

The second condition of equilibrium states that the sum of all torques acting on an object must also be zero for it to rotate with constant angular velocity; since the paratrooper is rotating with a constant angular velocity of 0.2 rad/sec, this condition is also satisfied. questions.text: Absolute zero is equivalent to:

Absolute zero is the lowest possible temperature that can be theoretically achieved. It is equal to 0 Kelvin (K), -273.15 degrees Celsius (°C), and -459.67 degrees Fahrenheit (°F). All three scales have their zero points set at different temperatures, but they converge at absolute zero. At this temperature, all molecular motion theoretically ceases, and no heat energy can be extracted from a system.

questions.text: The equation ΔU = ΔQ refers to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In an isothermal process, the temperature remains constant. The equation for an isothermal process is PV = constant, and the internal energy change (ΔU) is zero, not ΔU = ΔQ.
In an adiabatic process, no heat is exchanged with the surroundings (ΔQ = 0), leading to ΔU = -ΔW. Thus, ΔU = ΔQ is not applicable here.
In an isochoric process, the volume remains constant, which means the work done (W) is zero. Therefore, the change in internal energy (ΔU) is equal to the heat added or removed (ΔQ).
In an isobaric process, the pressure remains constant, and the equation becomes ΔU = ΔQ - ΔW. Therefore, ΔU = ΔQ does not apply as work done is not zero.
The Carnot cycle involves a series of processes including isothermal and adiabatic processes, where the net work done is not zero. Thus, ΔU = ΔQ does not apply to the entire cycle.

questions.text: The isothermal system has constant:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In an isothermal system, the temperature remains constant throughout the process. This is a fundamental characteristic of isothermal processes. The system is in thermal equilibrium with its surroundings, so there is no net transfer of heat, and thus the temperature remains constant.

In an isothermal process, the pressure of the system may change as the volume changes, but it is not necessarily constant. Isothermal processes occur at a constant temperature, but pressure may vary depending on the volume changes and the behavior of the gas.

Entropy may change in an isothermal process, especially if there is any irreversible work done or if there is a change in the number of microstates available to the system. While entropy tends to increase in many natural processes, it is not necessarily constant in an isothermal process.

While energy conservation is a fundamental principle in physics, it is not specific to isothermal processes. Energy conservation applies to all processes, including isothermal, adiabatic, and isobaric processes. However, in an isothermal process, the total internal energy of the system remains constant, as there is no change in temperature. This is a consequence of the first law of thermodynamics.

questions.text: Under which conditions, does a real gas approximate to an ideal gas?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When pressure and density are high, gas particles are closer together, increasing the likelihood of intermolecular interactions. At high pressures, the volume occupied by gas particles becomes significant compared to the volume of the container, and the assumption of negligible volume in the ideal gas law breaks down. Additionally, high pressure may lead to deviations from ideal behavior due to the compression of gas particles.

At low pressures but high densities, gas particles are still relatively close together, increasing the likelihood of intermolecular interactions. Although the volume occupied by gas particles may still be significant compared to the container's volume, the main factor here is the high density, which increases the chance of non-ideal behavior due to molecular interactions.

When pressure is high but density is low, gas particles are more spread out, reducing the frequency of intermolecular interactions. However, at high pressures, gas particles may be forced closer together, leading to deviations from ideal behavior due to increased molecular interactions. Moreover, low density does not necessarily guarantee ideal behavior, as it is the pressure-density combination that determines the extent of deviation from ideal gas behavior.

This combination of low pressure and low density is most conducive to approximating a real gas to an ideal gas. At low pressures, gas particles are relatively far apart, reducing the likelihood of intermolecular interactions. Similarly, low density implies that the volume occupied by gas particles is negligible compared to the container's volume, aligning with the assumptions of the ideal gas law. Under these conditions, the ideal gas law provides a good approximation of the behavior of the gas.

questions.explanation: Real gases approximate ideal gas behavior at relatively low density, low pressure, and high temperature. At high temperatures, the gas molecules have enough kinetic energy to overcome intermolecular forces, but at low temperatures, the gas has less kinetic energy and thus the intermolecular forces are more prominent.

options.explanation: Isochoric processes occur at constant volume. Isothermal processes occur at constant temperatures. While some biological processes may occur at relatively constant temperatures, many biological systems involve temperature changes due to metabolic activity or environmental factors. Therefore, while some biological processes may be isothermal, it is not a comprehensive description of how living systems work in general. However this is the closest possible answer.

Adiabatic processes occur without the transfer of heat between the system and its surroundings. While some biological processes may operate under conditions with minimal heat exchange, many biological systems do involve heat exchange with the environment. Isobaric processes occur at constant pressure. questions.text: Under a cyclic path, internal energy after the complete cycle is the same as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If a cyclic process is carried out on a system, the internal energy of the system will be the same at the end of the process as it was at the beginning of the process. If the cyclic process is reversible, then the internal energy of the system will be the same at the end of the process as it was at the beginning of the process. If the cyclic process is irreversible, then the internal energy of the system may not be the same at the end of the process as it was at the beginning of the process. However, the internal energy of a system will not be twice the initial value after a complete cycle.

The internal energy of a system is a state function, which means that it depends only on the initial and final states of the system and not on the path taken between those states. For a cyclic process, the system returns to its initial state, so the change in internal energy is zero. This means that the internal energy of the system after a complete cycle is the same as the internal energy of the system at the beginning of the cycle. It is not necessarily equal to the internal heat added to the system during the cycle.

According to the first law of thermodynamics, the internal energy of a system is conserved. This means that the internal energy of a system will be the same at the end of a complete cycle as it was initially, at the beginning of the cycle.

For a cyclic process, the net work done by the system is zero, so the work done by the system over a complete cycle is equal in magnitude but opposite in sign to the work done on the system during the reverse cycle. This means that the net work done by the system over a complete cycle is zero. So, initial work is not the same as internal energy.

questions.explanation: According to the first law of thermodynamics, the internal energy of a system is conserved. This means that the internal energy of a system will be the same at the end of a complete cycle as it was initially, at the beginning of the cycle.

questions.text: Read the passage. Then answer the questions below.Many people like to eat pizza, but not everyone knows how to make it. Making the perfect pizza can be complicated, but there are lots of ways for you to make a more basic version at home.When you make pizza, you must begin with the crust. The crust can be hard to make. If you want to make the crust yourself, you will have to make dough using flour, water, and yeast. You will have to knead the dough with your hands. If you do not have enough time to do this, you can use a prepared crust that you buy from the store.After you have chosen your crust, you must then add the sauce. Making your own sauce from scratch can take a long time. You have to buy tomatoes, peel them, and then cook them with spices. If this sounds like too much work, you can also purchase jarred sauce from the store. Many jarred sauces taste almost as good as the kind you make at home.Now that you have your crust and your sauce, you need to add the cheese. Cheese comes from milk, which comes from cows. Do you have a cow in your backyard? Do you know how to milk the cow?Do you know how to turn that milk into cheese? If not, you might want to buy cheese from the grocery store instead of making it yourself.When you have the crust, sauce, and cheese ready, you can add other toppings. Some people like to put meat on their pizza, while other people like to add vegetables. Some people even like to add pineapple! The best part of making a pizza at home is that you can customize it by adding your own favorite ingredients.Which of the following conclusions would work best at the end of this passage?

options.text: Although the crust, sauce, and toppings are all important ingredients in pizza, it is clear that the cheese is most important. Therefore, be sure your cheese is homemade

questions.explanation: A good conclusion restates the main idea and the basic points (or thesis) of the passage. In paragraph 1 the author introduces the idea of making a pizza at home.
